subject: Physics
title: Supercapacitors: Instant Energy
description: A short lesson to help you apply Supercapacitors: Instant Energy.

hook.text: Supercapacitors charge in seconds and last millions of cycles. They fill the gap between batteries and regular capacitors.

buildup.text: Regular capacitors store charge on plates. Supercapacitors use porous carbon with enormous surface area.

discovery.text: They store energy electrostatically—no chemical reactions. That's why they charge and discharge almost instantly.

twist.text: But they store far less energy than batteries. They're sprinters, not marathon runners.

climax.text: Buses in China use supercapacitors, recharging in 30 seconds at each stop—enough energy for the next stop.

punchline.text: Seconds to charge. Millions of cycles. Speed over stamina.

quiz.question: What advantage do supercapacitors have over batteries?
options: They charge and discharge almost instantly and last millions of cycles
They store more energy per kilogram
